text: Standing here, I sense a peculiar blend of anticipation and detachment. The first space, with scattered chairs and small clusters of conversation, feels transient—caught between gathering and dispersal. There is a hint of estrangement, as if everyone, myself included, is performing a role expected in this environment. The second space, awash in bright colors and definitive text, offers reflection—a mirror for the uncertainty I feel. The words about confusion, intention, and perception resonate within me, highlighting my internal search for meaning and belonging. In this overlap of public interaction and private thought, I hover somewhere in between, curious and unresolved.
